author: y4rr
content:
Architect-call escalation: max_abs<=0.5 floor is physically unsatisfiable for any bf16 fwd vs f32 ref.

Probe-before-fix control (transformers bf16 vs transformers f32, same prompt/model, SmolLM-135M):
  max_abs=1.4013, mean_abs=0.2187, cosine_sim=0.998864, top1=100%

Ferrotorch GPU bf16 vs transformers f32 (same prompt/model):
  max_abs=1.2205, mean_abs=0.1810, cosine_sim=0.999262, top1=100%, top5_overlap=4/5, last_match=True

Ferrotorch GPU is CLOSER to f32 reference than transformers bf16 (1.22 < 1.40, cosine 0.9993 > 0.9989). 1.22 max_abs is inherent bf16 noise compounded across 30 layers when logit magnitudes reach 27. NOT a ferrotorch bug.

Real bug surfaced + fixed during this work: LlamaGpuInferencer::new did NOT handle tie_word_embeddings — SmolLM-135M (tie=true) failed with 'missing tensor lm_head.weight'. CPU path's load_hf_state_dict already handles this; GPU path now matches.

Need architect decision on:
  (a) raise floor to max_abs<=1.5 (physical bf16 budget validated by transformers bf16 control), keep cosine_sim>=0.99 + top1>=99% as-is
  (b) switch reference to ferrotorch CPU f32 (matches our op-order)
  (c) keep harness as documented FAIL

Files added/modified, all builds clean (cuda lib tests 134 pass, cpu lib tests 103 pass, clippy clean):
  M ferrotorch-llama/src/gpu.rs           — tied-embeddings fix + forward_logits_from_ids_all
  M ferrotorch-llama/examples/llm_inference_dump.rs — --device gpu flag
  A ferrotorch-llama/tests/conformance_pretrained_causal_lm_gpu.rs — cargo test wrapper (CUDA-skip-graceful)
  A scripts/verify_causal_lm_gpu_inference.py — GPU harness

NOT committed pending architect decision.
